1:
Happy are those
who reject the advice of evil people,
who do not follow the example of sinners
or join those who have no use for God.
True Happiness
2:
Instead, they find joy in obeying the Law of the 
LORD
,
and they study it day and night.
True Happiness
3:
They are like trees that grow beside a stream,
that bear fruit at the right time,
and whose leaves do not dry up.
They succeed in everything they do.
True Happiness
4:
But evil people are not like this at all;
they are like straw that the wind blows away.
True Happiness
5:
Sinners will be condemned by God
and kept apart from God's own people.
True Happiness
6:
The righteous are guided and protected by the 
LORD
,
but the evil are on the way to their doom.
True Happiness

1:
Why do the nations plan rebellion?
Why do people make their useless plots?
God's Chosen King
2:
Their kings revolt,
their rulers plot together against the 
LORD
and against the king he chose.
God's Chosen King
3:
“Let us free ourselves from their rule,” they say;
“let us throw off their control.”
God's Chosen King
4:
From his throne in heaven the Lord laughs
and mocks their feeble plans.
God's Chosen King
5:
Then he warns them in anger
and terrifies them with his fury.
God's Chosen King
6:
“On Zion,
 my sacred hill,” he says,
“I have installed my king.”
God's Chosen King
; 2.6 Zion: See Zion in Word List.
7:
“I will announce,” says the king, “what the 
LORD
 has declared.
He said to me: ‘You are my son;
today I have become your father.
God's Chosen King
8:
Ask, and I will give you all the nations;
the whole earth will be yours.
God's Chosen King
9:
You will break them with an iron rod;
you will shatter them in pieces like a clay pot.’ ”
God's Chosen King
10:
Now listen to this warning, you kings;
learn this lesson, you rulers of the world:
God's Chosen King
11: God's Chosen King
12:
tremble and bow down to him;
or else his anger will be quickly aroused,
and you will suddenly die.
Happy are all who go to him for protection.
God's Chosen King
; 2.12 Probable text tremble… him; some other possible texts with trembling kiss his feet and with trembling kiss the Son and tremble and kiss the mighty one; Hebrew unclear.

Remember that the 
LORD
 has chosen the righteous for his own,

The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
8:
Protect me as you would your very eyes;
hide me in the shadow of your wings
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
9:
from the attacks of the wicked.
Deadly enemies surround me;
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
10: they have no pity and speak proudly.
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
11:
They are round me now, wherever I turn,
watching for a chance to pull me down.
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
12:
They are like lions, waiting for me,
wanting to tear me to pieces.
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
13:
Come, 
LORD
! Oppose my enemies and defeat them!
Save me from the wicked by your sword;
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
14:
save me from those who in this life have all they want.
Punish them with the sufferings you have stored up for them;
may there be enough for their children
and some left over for their children's children!
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.
15:
But I will see you, because I have done no wrong;
and when I awake, your presence will fill me with joy.
The Prayer of an Innocent Person#Ps 17 Hebrew title: A prayer by David.

1:
How I love you, 
LORD
!
You are my defender.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
2:
The 
LORD
 is my protector;
he is my strong fortress.
My God is my protection,
and with him I am safe.
He protects me like a shield;
he defends me and keeps me safe.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
3:
I call to the 
LORD
,
and he saves me from my enemies.
Praise the 
LORD
!
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
4:
The danger of death was all round me;
the waves of destruction rolled over me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
5:
The danger of death was round me,
and the grave set its trap for me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
6: David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
7: David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
8:
Smoke poured out of his nostrils,
a consuming flame and burning coals from his mouth.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
9:
He tore the sky apart and came down
with a dark cloud under his feet.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
10:
He flew swiftly on a winged creature;
he travelled on the wings of the wind.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
; 18.10 winged creature: See Word List.
11:
He covered himself with darkness;
thick clouds, full of water, surrounded him.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
12:
Hailstones and flashes of fire
came from the lightning before him
and broke through the dark clouds.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
13:
Then the 
LORD
 thundered from the sky;
and the voice of the Most High was heard.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
; 18.13 One ancient translation (and see 2 Sam 22.14) was heard; Hebrew was heard hailstones and flashes of fire.
14:
He shot his arrows and scattered his enemies;
with flashes of lightning he sent them running.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
15:
The floor of the ocean was laid bare,
and the foundations of the earth were uncovered,
when you rebuked your enemies, 
LORD
,
and roared at them in anger.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
16:
The 
LORD
 reached down from above and took hold of me;
he pulled me out of the deep waters.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
17:
He rescued me from my powerful enemies
and from all those who hate me —
they were too strong for me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
18:
When I was in trouble, they attacked me,
but the 
LORD
 protected me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
19:
He helped me out of danger;
he saved me because he was pleased with me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
20:
The 
LORD
 rewards me because I do what is right;
he blesses me because I am innocent.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
21:
I have obeyed the law of the 
LORD
;
I have not turned away from my God.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
22:
I have observed all his laws;
I have not disobeyed his commands.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
23:
He knows that I am faultless,
that I have kept myself from doing wrong.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
24:
And so he rewards me because I do what is right,
because he knows that I am innocent.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
25:
O 
LORD
, you are faithful to those who are faithful to you;
completely good to those who are perfect.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
26:
You are pure to those who are pure,
but hostile to those who are wicked.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
27:
You save those who are humble,
but you humble those who are proud.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
28:
O 
LORD
, you give me light;
you dispel my darkness.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
29:
You give me strength to attack my enemies
and power to overcome their defences.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
30:
This God — how perfect are his deeds!
How dependable his words!
He is like a shield
for all who seek his protection.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
31:
The 
LORD
 alone is God;
God alone is our defence.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
32:
He is the God who makes me strong,
who makes my pathway safe.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
33:
He makes me sure-footed as a deer;
he keeps me safe on the mountains.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
34:
He trains me for battle,
so that I can use the strongest bow.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
35:
O 
LORD
, you protect me and save me;
your care has made me great,
and your power has kept me safe.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
36:
You have kept me from being captured,
and I have never fallen.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
37:
I pursue my enemies and catch them;
I do not stop until I destroy them.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
38:
I strike them down, and they cannot rise;
they lie defeated before me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
39:
You give me strength for the battle
and victory over my enemies.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
40:
You make my enemies run from me;
I destroy those who hate me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
41:
They cry for help, but no one saves them;
they call to the 
LORD
, but he does not answer.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
42:
I crush them, so that they become like dust
which the wind blows away.
I trample on them like mud in the streets.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
43: David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
44:
Foreigners bow before me;
when they hear me, they obey.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
45:
They lose their courage
and come trembling from their fortresses.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
46:
The 
LORD
 lives! Praise my defender!
Proclaim the greatness of the God who saves me.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
47:
He gives me victory over my enemies;
he subdues the nations under me
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
48:
and saves me from my foes.
O 
LORD
, you give me victory over my enemies
and protect me from violent people.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.
49:
And so I praise you among the nations;
I sing praises to you.
David's Song of Victory#Ps 18 Hebrew title: The words that David, the LORD's servant, sang to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from Saul and all his other enemies.

1:
How clearly the sky reveals God's glory!
How plainly it shows what he has done!
God's Glory in Creation#Ps 19 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
Each day announces it to the following day;
each night repeats it to the next.
God's Glory in Creation#Ps 19 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
No speech or words are used,
no sound is heard;
God's Glory in Creation#Ps 19 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
yet their message
 goes out to all the world
and is heard to the ends of the earth.
God made a home in the sky for the sun;
God's Glory in Creation#Ps 19 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
19.4 Some ancient translations message; Hebrew line.; ; Rom 10.18
5:
it comes out in the morning like a happy bridegroom,
like an athlete eager to run a race.
God's Glory in Creation#Ps 19 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
It starts at one end of the sky
and goes across to the other.
Nothing can hide from its heat.
The Law of the LORD
7:
The law of the 
LORD
 is perfect;
it gives new strength.
The commands of the 
LORD
 are trustworthy,
giving wisdom to those who lack it.
The Law of the LORD
8:
The laws of the 
LORD
 are right,
and those who obey them are happy.
The commands of the 
LORD
 are just
and give understanding to the mind.
The Law of the LORD
9:
Reverence for the 
LORD
 is good;
it will continue for ever.
The judgements of the 
LORD
 are just;
they are always fair.
The Law of the LORD
10:
They are more desirable than the finest gold;
they are sweeter than the purest honey.
The Law of the LORD
11:
They give knowledge to me, your servant;
I am rewarded for obeying them.
The Law of the LORD
12:
No one can see his own errors;
deliver me, 
LORD
, from hidden faults!
The Law of the LORD
13:
Keep me safe, also, from wilful sins;
don't let them rule over me.
Then I shall be perfect
and free from the evil of sin.
The Law of the LORD
14:
May my words and my thoughts be acceptable to you,
O 
LORD
, my refuge and my redeemer!
The Law of the LORD

1:
May the 
LORD
 answer you when you are in trouble!
May the God of Jacob protect you!
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
May he send you help from his Temple
and give you aid from Mount Zion.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
May he accept all your offerings
and be pleased with all your sacrifices.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
May he give you what you desire
and make all your plans succeed.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
Then we will shout for joy over your victory
and celebrate your triumph by praising our God.
May the 
LORD
 answer all your requests.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
Now I know that the 
LORD
 gives victory to his chosen king;
he answers him from his holy heaven
and by his power gives him great victories.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
Some trust in their war chariots
and others in their horses,
but we trust in the power of the 
LORD
 our God.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
Such people will stumble and fall,
but we will rise and stand firm.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
Give victory to the king, O 
LORD
;
answer
 us when we call.
A Prayer for Victory#Ps 20 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 20.9 Some ancient translations answer; Hebrew he will answer.

1:
The king is glad, O 
LORD
, because you gave him strength;
he rejoices because you made him victorious.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
You have given him his heart's desire;
you have answered his request.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
You came to him with great blessings
and set a crown of gold on his head.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
He asked for life, and you gave it,
a long and lasting life.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
His glory is great because of your help;
you have given him fame and majesty.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
Your blessings are with him for ever
and your presence fills him with joy.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
The king trusts in the 
LORD
 Almighty;
and because of the 
LORD
's constant love
he will always be secure.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
The king will capture all his enemies;
he will capture everyone who hates him.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
He will destroy them like a blazing fire
when he appears.
The 
LORD
 will devour them in his anger,
and fire will consume them.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
None of their descendants will survive;
the king will kill them all.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
They make their plans, and plot against him,
but they will not succeed.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12:
He will shoot his arrows at them
and make them turn and run.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
13:
We praise you, 
LORD
, for your great strength!
We will sing and praise your power.
Praise for Victory#Ps 21 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?
I have cried desperately for help,
but still it does not come.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
During the day I call to you, my God,
but you do not answer;
I call at night,
but get no rest.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
But you are enthroned as the Holy One,
the one whom Israel praises.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
Our ancestors put their trust in you;
they trusted you, and you saved them.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
They called to you and escaped from danger;
they trusted you and were not disappointed.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
But I am no longer a human being; I am a worm,
despised and scorned by everyone!
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
All who see me jeer at me;
they stick out their tongues and shake their heads.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
“You relied on the 
LORD
,” they say.
“Why doesn't he save you?
If the 
LORD
 likes you,
why doesn't he help you?”
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
It was you who brought me safely through birth,
and when I was a baby, you kept me safe.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
I have relied on you since the day I was born,
and you have always been my God.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
Do not stay away from me!
Trouble is near,
and there is no one to help.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12: A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
13:
They open their mouths like lions,
roaring and tearing at me.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
14:
My strength is gone,
gone like water spilt on the ground.
All my bones are out of joint;
my heart is like melted wax.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
15:
My throat
 is as dry as dust,
and my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth.
You have left me for dead in the dust.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 22.15 Probable text throat; Hebrew strength.
16: A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 22.16 Some ancient translations they tear at; others they tie; Hebrew like a lion.
17:
All my bones can be seen.
My enemies look at me and stare.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
18:
They gamble for my clothes
and divide them among themselves.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
19:
O 
LORD
, don't stay away from me!
Come quickly to my rescue!
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
20:
Save me from the sword;
save my life from these dogs.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
21: A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 22.21 Some ancient translations I am helpless; Hebrew you answered me.
22:
I will tell my people what you have done;
I will praise you in their assembly:
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
23:
“Praise him, you servants of the 
LORD
!
Honour him, you descendants of Jacob!
Worship him, you people of Israel!
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
24: A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
25: A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
26:
The poor will eat as much as they want;
those who come to the 
LORD
 will praise him.
May they prosper for ever!
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
27:
All nations will remember the 
LORD
.
From every part of the world they will turn to him;
all races will worship him.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
28:
The 
LORD
 is king,
and he rules the nations.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
29:
All proud people will bow down to him;
all mortals will bow down before him.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 22.29 Probable text will bow down to him; Hebrew will eat and bow down.
30:
Future generations will serve him;
they will speak of the Lord to the coming generation.
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
31:
People not yet born will be told:
“The Lord saved his people.”
A Cry of Anguish and a Song of Praise#Ps 22 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
The 
LORD
 is my shepherd;
I have everything I need.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
He lets me rest in fields of green grass
and leads me to quiet pools of fresh water.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
He gives me new strength.
He guides me in the right paths,
as he has promised.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
Even if I go through the deepest darkness,
I will not be afraid, 
LORD
,
for you are with me.
Your shepherd's rod and staff protect me.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
You prepare a banquet for me,
where all my enemies can see me;
you welcome me as an honoured guest
and fill my cup to the brim.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
I know that your goodness and love will be with me all my life;
and your house will be my home as long as I live.
The LORD our Shepherd#Ps 23 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
The world and all that is in it belong to the 
LORD
;
the earth and all who live on it are his.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
He built it on the deep waters beneath the earth
and laid its foundations in the ocean depths.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
Who has the right to go up the 
LORD
's hill?
Who may enter his holy Temple?
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 24.3 the LORD's hill: The hill in Jerusalem on which the Temple was built.
4:
Those who are pure in act and in thought,
who do not worship idols
or make false promises.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
The 
LORD
 will bless them and save them;
God will declare them innocent.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
Such are the people who come to God,
who come into the presence of the God of Jacob.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
Fling wide the gates,
open the ancient doors,
and the great king will come in.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
Who is this great king?
He is the 
LORD
, strong and mighty,
the 
LORD
, victorious in battle.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
Fling wide the gates,
open the ancient doors,
and the great king will come in.
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
Who is this great king?
The triumphant 
LORD
 — he is the great king!
The Great King#Ps 24 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1: A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
2: A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Defeat does not come to those who trust in you,
but to those who are quick to rebel against you.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
Teach me your ways, O 
LORD
;
make them known to me.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
Teach me to live according to your truth,
for you are my God, who saves me.
I always trust in you.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
Remember, O 
LORD
, your kindness and constant love
which you have shown from long ago.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
Forgive the sins and errors of my youth.
In your constant love and goodness,
remember me, 
LORD
!
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
Because the 
LORD
 is righteous and good,
he teaches sinners the path they should follow.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
He leads the humble in the right way
and teaches them his will.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
10:
With faithfulness and love he leads
all who keep his covenant and obey his commands.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
11:
Keep your promise, 
LORD
, and forgive my sins,
for they are many.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
Those who have reverence for the 
LORD
will learn from him the path they should follow.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
13:
They will always be prosperous,
and their children will possess the land.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
14:
The 
LORD
 is the friend of those who obey him
and he affirms his covenant with them.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
15:
I look to the 
LORD
 for help at all times,
and he rescues me from danger.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
16:
Turn to me, 
LORD
, and be merciful to me,
because I am lonely and weak.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
17:
Relieve me of my worries
and save me from all my troubles.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
18:
Consider my distress and suffering
and forgive all my sins.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
19:
See how many enemies I have;
see how much they hate me.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
20: A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
21:
May my goodness and honesty preserve me,
because I trust in you.
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.
22:
From all their troubles, O God,
save your people Israel!
A Prayer for Guidance and Protection#Ps 25 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
Declare me innocent, O 
LORD
,
because I do what is right
and trust you completely.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
Examine me and test me, 
LORD
;
judge my desires and thoughts.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Your constant love is my guide;
your faithfulness always leads me.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
; 26.3 your faithfulness always leads me; or I live in loyalty to you.
4:
I do not keep company with worthless people;
I have nothing to do with hypocrites.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
I hate the company of the evil
and avoid the wicked.
LORD
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
, I wash my hands to show that I am innocent
and march in worship round your altar.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
I sing a hymn of thanksgiving
and tell of all your wonderful deeds.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
I love the house where you live, O 
LORD
,
the place where your glory dwells.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
Do not destroy me with the sinners;
spare me from the fate of murderers —
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
10:
those who do evil all the time
and are always ready to take bribes.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
11:
As for me, I do what is right;
be merciful to me and save me!
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
I am safe from all dangers;
in the assembly of his people I praise the 
LORD
.
The Prayer of a Good Person#Ps 26 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
The 
LORD
 is my light and my salvation;
I will fear no one.
The 
LORD
 protects me from all danger;
I will never be afraid.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
When evil people attack me and try to kill me,
they stumble and fall.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Even if a whole army surrounds me,
I will not be afraid;
even if enemies attack me,
I will still trust God.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
; 27.3 still trust God; or not lose courage.
4:
I have asked the 
LORD
 for one thing;
one thing only do I want:
to live in the 
LORD
's house all my life,
to marvel there at his goodness,
and to ask for his guidance.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
5: A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
So I will triumph over my enemies around me.
With shouts of joy I will offer sacrifices in his Temple;
I will sing, I will praise the 
LORD
.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
Hear me, 
LORD
, when I call to you!
Be merciful and answer me!
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
When you said, “Come and worship me,”
I answered, “I will come, 
LORD
;
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
9: A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
10:
My father and mother may abandon me,
but the 
LORD
 will take care of me.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
11:
Teach me, 
LORD
, what you want me to do,
and lead me along a safe path,
because I have many enemies.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
Don't abandon me to my enemies,
who attack me with lies and threats.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
13:
I know that I will live to see
the 
LORD
's goodness in this present life.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.
14:
Trust in the 
LORD
.
Have faith, do not despair.
Trust in the 
LORD
.
A Prayer of Praise#Ps 27 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
O 
LORD
, my defender, I call to you.
Listen to my cry!
If you do not answer me,
I will be among those who go down to the world of the dead.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
Hear me when I cry to you for help,
when I lift my hands towards your holy Temple.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Do not condemn me with the wicked,
with those who do evil —
those whose words are friendly,
but who have hatred in their hearts.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
Punish them for what they have done,
for the evil they have committed.
Punish them for all their deeds;
give them what they deserve!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
They take no notice of what the 
LORD
 has done
or of what he has made;
so he will punish them
and destroy them for ever.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
Give praise to the 
LORD
;
he has heard my cry for help.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
7: A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
The 
LORD
 protects his people;
he defends and saves his chosen king.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
Save your people, 
LORD
,
and bless those who are yours.
Be their shepherd,
and take care of them for ever.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 28 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
Praise the 
LORD
, you heavenly beings;
praise his glory and power.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
Praise the 
LORD
's glorious name;
bow down before the Holy One when he appears.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 29.2 when he appears; or in garments of worship; or in his beautiful Temple.
3:
The voice of the 
LORD
 is heard on the seas;
the glorious God thunders,
and his voice echoes over the ocean.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
The voice of the 
LORD
 is heard
in all its might and majesty.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
The voice of the 
LORD
 breaks the cedars,
even the cedars of Lebanon.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
He makes the mountains of Lebanon jump like calves
and makes Mount Hermon leap like a young bull.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
The voice of the 
LORD
 makes the lightning flash.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
His voice makes the desert shake;
he shakes the desert of Kadesh.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
The 
LORD
's voice shakes the oaks
and strips the leaves from the trees
while everyone in his Temple shouts, “Glory to God!”
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 29.9 Probable text shakes the oaks; Hebrew makes the deer give birth.
10:
The 
LORD
 rules over the deep waters;
he rules as king for ever.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
The 
LORD
 gives strength to his people
and blesses them with peace.
The Voice of the LORD in the Storm#Ps 29 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
I praise you, 
LORD
, because you have saved me
and kept my enemies from gloating over me.
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
2:
I cried to you for help, O 
LORD
 my God,
and you healed me;
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
3:
you kept me from the grave.
I was on my way to the depths below,
but you restored my life.
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
; 30.3 the depths below: The world of the dead (see 6.5).
4:
Sing praise to the 
LORD
,
all his faithful people!
Remember what the Holy One has done,
and give him thanks!
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
5:
His anger lasts only a moment,
his goodness for a lifetime.
Tears may flow in the night,
but joy comes in the morning.
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
6:
I felt secure and said to myself,
“I will never be defeated.”
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
7:
You were good to me, 
LORD
;
you protected me like a mountain fortress.
But then you hid yourself from me,
and I was afraid.
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
8:
I called to you, 
LORD
;
I begged for your help:
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
9:
“What will you gain from my death?
What profit from my going to the grave?
Are dead people able to praise you?
Can they proclaim your unfailing goodness?
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
10:
Hear me, 
LORD
, and be merciful!
Help me, 
LORD
!”
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
11: A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.
12:
So I will not be silent;
I will sing praise to you.
LORD
, you are my God,
I will give you thanks for ever.
A Prayer of Thanksgiving#Ps 30 Hebrew title: A song for the dedication of the Temple; a psalm by David.

1: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
You are my refuge and defence;
guide me and lead me as you have promised.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
Keep me safe from the trap that has been set for me;
shelter me from danger.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
I place myself in your care.
You will save me, 
LORD
;
you are a faithful God.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
You hate those who worship false gods,
but I trust in you.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
I will be glad and rejoice
because of your constant love.
You see my suffering;
you know my trouble.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
You have not let my enemies capture me;
you have given me freedom to go where I wish.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
I am exhausted by sorrow,
and weeping has shortened my life.
I am weak from all my troubles;
even my bones are wasting away.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 31.10 Some ancient translations troubles; Hebrew iniquity.
11: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12:
Everyone has forgotten me, as though I were dead;
I am like something thrown away.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
13:
I hear many enemies whispering;
terror is all round me.
They are making plans against me,
plotting to kill me.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
14:
But my trust is in you, O 
LORD
;
you are my God.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
15: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
16:
Look on your servant with kindness;
save me in your constant love.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
17: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
18:
Silence those liars —
all the proud and arrogant
who speak with contempt about the righteous.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
19:
How wonderful are the good things
you keep for those who honour you!
Everyone knows how good you are,
how securely you protect those who trust you.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
20:
You hide them in the safety of your presence
from the plots of others;
in a safe shelter you hide them
from the insults of their enemies.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
21:
Praise the 
LORD
!
How wonderfully he showed his love for me
when I was surrounded and attacked!
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
22:
I was afraid and thought
that he had driven me out of his presence.
But he heard my cry,
when I called to him for help.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
23:
Love the 
LORD
, all his faithful people.
The 
LORD
 protects the faithful,
but punishes the proud as they deserve.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
24:
Be strong, be courageous,
all you that hope in the 
LORD
.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 31 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
Happy are those whose sins are forgiven,
whose wrongs are pardoned.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
2:
Happy is the one whom the 
LORD
 does not accuse of doing wrong
and who is free from all deceit.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
3:
When I did not confess my sins,
I was worn out from crying all day long.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
4:
Day and night you punished me, 
LORD
;
my strength was completely drained,
as moisture is dried up by the summer heat.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
5:
Then I confessed my sins to you;
I did not conceal my wrongdoings.
I decided to confess them to you,
and you forgave all my sins.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
6: Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
; 32.6 Some ancient translations need; Hebrew finding only.
7:
You are my hiding place;
you will save me from trouble.
I sing aloud of your salvation,
because you protect me.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
8:
The 
LORD
 says, “I will teach you the way you should go;
I will instruct you and advise you.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
9:
Don't be stupid like a horse or a mule,
which must be controlled with a bit and bridle
to make it submit.”
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
10:
The wicked will have to suffer,
but those who trust in the 
LORD
are protected by his constant love.
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
11:
You that are righteous, be glad and rejoice
because of what the 
LORD
 has done.
You that obey him, shout for joy!
Confession and Forgiveness#Ps 32 Hebrew title: A poem by David.

1:
All you that are righteous,
shout for joy for what the 
LORD
 has done;
praise him, all you that obey him.
A Song of Praise
2:
Give thanks to the 
LORD
 with harps,
sing to him with stringed instruments.
A Song of Praise
3:
Sing a new song to him,
play the harp with skill, and shout for joy!
A Song of Praise
4:
The words of the 
LORD
 are true
and all his works are dependable.
A Song of Praise
5:
The 
LORD
 loves what is righteous and just;
his constant love fills the earth.
A Song of Praise
6:
The 
LORD
 created the heavens by his command,
the sun, moon, and stars by his spoken word.
A Song of Praise
7:
He gathered all the seas into one place;
he shut up the ocean depths in storerooms.
A Song of Praise
8:
Worship the 
LORD
, all the earth!
Honour him, all peoples of the world!
A Song of Praise
9:
When he spoke, the world was created;
at his command everything appeared.
A Song of Praise
10:
The 
LORD
 frustrates the purposes of the nations;
he keeps them from carrying out their plans.
A Song of Praise
11:
But his plans endure for ever;
his purposes last eternally.
A Song of Praise
12:
Happy is the nation whose God is the 
LORD
;
happy are the people he has chosen for his own!
A Song of Praise
13:
The 
LORD
 looks down from heaven
and sees the whole human race.
A Song of Praise
14:
From where he rules, he looks down
on all who live on earth.
A Song of Praise
15:
He forms all their thoughts
and knows everything they do.
A Song of Praise
16:
A king does not win because of his powerful army;
a soldier does not triumph because of his strength.
A Song of Praise
17:
War horses are useless for victory;
their great strength cannot save.
A Song of Praise
18:
The 
LORD
 watches over those who obey him,
those who trust in his constant love.
A Song of Praise
19:
He saves them from death;
he keeps them alive in times of famine.
A Song of Praise
20:
We put our hope in the 
LORD
;
he is our protector and our help.
A Song of Praise
21:
We are glad because of him;
we trust in his holy name.
A Song of Praise
22:
May your constant love be with us, 
LORD
,
as we put our hope in you.
A Song of Praise

1:
I will always thank the 
LORD
;
I will never stop praising him.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
2:
I will praise him for what he has done;
may all who are oppressed listen and be glad!
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
3:
Proclaim with me the 
LORD
's greatness;
let us praise his name together!
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
4:
I prayed to the 
LORD
, and he answered me;
he freed me from all my fears.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
5:
The oppressed look to him and are glad;
they will never be disappointed.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
6:
The helpless call to him, and he answers;
he saves them from all their troubles.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
7:
His angel guards those who honour the 
LORD
and rescues them from danger.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
8:
Find out for yourself how good the 
LORD
 is.
Happy are those who find safety with him.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
9:
Honour the 
LORD
, all his people;
those who obey him have all they need.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
10:
Even lions go hungry for lack of food,
but those who obey the 
LORD
 lack nothing good.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
11:
Come, my young friends, and listen to me,
and I will teach you to honour the 
LORD
.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
12:
Would you like to enjoy life?
Do you want long life and happiness?
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
13:
Then hold back from speaking evil
and from telling lies.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
14:
Turn away from evil and do good;
strive for peace with all your heart.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
15:
The 
LORD
 watches over the righteous
and listens to their cries;
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
16:
but he opposes those who do evil,
so that when they die, they are soon forgotten.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
17:
The righteous call to the 
LORD
, and he listens;
he rescues them from all their troubles.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
18:
The 
LORD
 is near to those who are discouraged;
he saves those who have lost all hope.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
19:
Good people suffer many troubles,
but the 
LORD
 saves them from them all;
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
20:
the 
LORD
 preserves them completely;
not one of their bones is broken.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
21:
Evil will kill the wicked;
those who hate the righteous will be punished.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15
22:
The 
LORD
 will save his people;
those who go to him for protection will be spared.
In Praise of God's Goodness#Ps 34 Hebrew title: By David, who left the presence of Abimelech after pretending to be mad and being sent away by him.#1 Sam 21.13–15

1:
Oppose those who oppose me, 
LORD
,
and fight those who fight against me!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
Take your shield and armour
and come to my rescue.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Lift up your spear and your axe
against those who pursue me.
Promise that you will save me.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
May those who try to kill me
be defeated and disgraced!
May those who plot against me
be turned back and confused!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
May they be like straw blown by the wind
as the angel of the 
LORD
 pursues them!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
May their path be dark and slippery
while the angel of the 
LORD
 strikes them down!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
Without any reason they laid a trap for me
and dug a deep hole to catch me.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
8: A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
Then I will be glad because of the 
LORD
;
I will be happy because he saved me.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
10:
With all my heart I will say to the 
LORD
,
“There is no one like you.
You protect the weak from the strong,
the poor from the oppressor.”
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
11:
Evil people testify against me
and accuse me of crimes I know nothing about.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
They pay me back evil for good,
and I sink in despair.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
13: A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
14:
as I would pray for a friend or a brother.
I went about bent over in mourning,
as one who mourns for his mother.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
15:
But when I was in trouble, they were all glad
and gathered round to mock me;
strangers beat me
and kept striking me.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
16:
Like those who would mock a cripple,
they glared at me with hate.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
; 35.16 Probable text Like those… cripple; Hebrew unclear.
17: A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
18:
Then I will thank you in the assembly of your people;
I will praise you before them all.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
19:
Don't let my enemies, those liars,
gloat over my defeat.
Don't let those who hate me for no reason
smirk with delight over my sorrow.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
20:
They do not speak in a friendly way;
instead they invent all kinds of lies about peace-loving people.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
21:
They accuse me, shouting,
“We saw what you did!”
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
22:
But you, O 
LORD
, have seen this.
So don't be silent, Lord;
don't keep yourself far away!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
23:
Rouse yourself, O Lord, and defend me;
rise up, my God, and plead my cause.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
24:
You are righteous, O 
LORD
, so declare me innocent;
don't let my enemies gloat over me.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
25:
Don't let them say to themselves,
“We are rid of him!
That's just what we wanted!”
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
26:
May those who gloat over my suffering
be completely defeated and confused;
may those who claim to be better than I am
be covered with shame and disgrace.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
27:
May those who want to see me acquitted
shout for joy and say again and again,
“How great is the 
LORD
!
He is pleased with the success of his servant.”
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.
28:
Then I will proclaim your righteousness,
and I will praise you all day long.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 35 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
Sin speaks to the wicked deep in their hearts;
they reject God and have no reverence for him.
Human Wickedness#Ps 36 Hebrew title: By David, the LORD's servant.
2:
Because they think so highly of themselves,
they think that God will not discover their sin and condemn it.
Human Wickedness#Ps 36 Hebrew title: By David, the LORD's servant.
3:
Their speech is wicked and full of lies;
they no longer do what is wise and good.
Human Wickedness#Ps 36 Hebrew title: By David, the LORD's servant.
4:
They make evil plans as they lie in bed;
nothing they do is good,
and they never reject anything evil.
LORD
The Goodness of God
5:
, your constant love reaches the heavens;
your faithfulness extends to the skies.
The Goodness of God
6: The Goodness of God
7:
How precious, O God, is your constant love!
We find
 protection under the shadow of your wings.
The Goodness of God
; 36.7 precious, O God, is… find; or precious is your constant love! Gods and people find.
8:
We feast on the abundant food you provide;
you let us drink from the river of your goodness.
The Goodness of God
9:
You are the source of all life,
and because of your light we see the light.
The Goodness of God
10:
Continue to love those who know you
and to do good to those who are righteous.
The Goodness of God
11:
Do not let proud people attack me
or the wicked make me run away.
The Goodness of God
12:
See where evil people have fallen.
There they lie, unable to rise.
The Goodness of God

1:
Don't be worried on account of the wicked;
don't be jealous of those who do wrong.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
They will soon disappear like grass that dries up;
they will die like plants that wither.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
Trust in the 
LORD
 and do good;
live in the land and be safe.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
Seek your happiness in the 
LORD
,
and he will give you your heart's desire.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
5: The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
6: he will make your righteousness shine like the noonday sun.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
Be patient and wait for the 
LORD
 to act;
don't be worried about those who prosper
or those who succeed in their evil plans.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
Don't give in to worry or anger;
it only leads to trouble.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
Those who trust in the 
LORD
 will possess the land,
but the wicked will be driven out.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
10:
Soon the wicked will disappear;
you may look for them, but you won't find them;
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
11:
the humble will possess the land
and enjoy prosperity and peace.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
The wicked plot against good people
and glare at them with hate.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
13:
But the Lord laughs at wicked people,
because he knows they will soon be destroyed.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
14: The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
15:
but they will be killed by their own swords,
and their bows will be smashed.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
16:
The little that a good person owns
is worth more than the wealth of all the wicked,
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
17:
because the 
LORD
 will take away the strength of the wicked,
but protect those who are good.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
18:
The 
LORD
 takes care of those who obey him,
and the land will be theirs for ever.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
19:
They will not suffer when times are bad;
they will have enough in time of famine.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
20: The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
21:
The wicked borrow and never pay back,
but good people are generous with their gifts.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
22:
Those who are blessed by the 
LORD
 will possess the land,
but those who are cursed by him will be driven out.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
23:
The 
LORD
 guides people in the way they should go
and protects those who please him.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
24:
If they fall, they will not stay down,
because the 
LORD
 will help them up.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
25:
I am old now; I have lived a long time,
but I have never seen good people abandoned by the 
LORD
or their children begging for food.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
26:
At all times they give freely and lend to others,
and their children are a blessing.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
27:
Turn away from evil and do good,
and your descendants will always live in the land;
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
28:
for the 
LORD
 loves what is right
and does not abandon his faithful people.
He protects them for ever,
but the descendants of the wicked will be driven out.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
29:
The righteous will possess the land
and live in it for ever.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
30:
The words of good people are wise,
and they are always fair.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
31:
They keep the law of their God in their hearts
and never depart from it.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
32:
Wicked people watch good people
and try to kill them;
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
33:
but the 
LORD
 will not abandon them to their enemy's power
or let them be condemned when they are on trial.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
34:
Put your hope in the 
LORD
 and obey his commands;
he will honour you by giving you the land,
and you will see the wicked driven out.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.

I once knew a wicked and ruthless person;

36: The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.

Notice good people, observe the righteous;
peaceful people have descendants,
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
38:
but sinners are completely destroyed,
and their descendants are wiped out.
The Destiny of the Wicked and of the Good#Ps 37 Hebrew title: By David.
39:
The 
LORD
 saves the righteous
and protects them in times of trouble.

1:
O 
LORD
, don't punish me in your anger!
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
2:
You have wounded me with your arrows;
you have struck me down.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
3:
Because of your anger, I am in great pain;
my whole body is diseased because of my sins.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
4:
I am drowning in the flood of my sins;
they are a burden too heavy to bear.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
5:
Because I have been foolish,
my sores stink and rot.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
6:
I am bowed down, I am crushed;
I mourn all day long.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
7:
I am burning with fever
and I am near to death.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
8:
I am worn out and utterly crushed;
my heart is troubled, and I groan with pain.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
9:
O Lord, you know what I long for;
you hear all my groans.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
10:
My heart is pounding, my strength is gone,
and my eyes have lost their brightness.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
11: The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
12: The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
13:
I am like the deaf and cannot hear,
like the dumb and cannot speak.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
14:
I am like those who do not answer,
because they cannot hear.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
15:
But I trust in you, O 
LORD
;
and you, O Lord my God, will answer me.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
16:
Don't let my enemies gloat over my distress;
don't let them boast about my downfall!
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
17:
I am about to fall
and am in constant pain.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
18:
I confess my sins;
they fill me with anxiety.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
19:
My enemies are healthy and strong;
there are many who hate me for no reason.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
20:
Those who pay back evil for good
are against me because I try to do right.
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
21:
Do not abandon me, O 
LORD
;
do not stay away, my God!
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
22: Help me now, O Lord my saviour!
The Prayer of a Sufferer#Ps 38 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.

1:
I said, “I will be careful what I do
and will not let my tongue make me sin;
I will not say anything
while evil people are near.”
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
I kept quiet, not saying a word,
not even about anything good!
But my suffering only grew worse,
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3: The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
“
LORD
, how long will I live?
When will I die?
Tell me how soon my life will end.”
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
How short you have made my life!
In your sight my lifetime seems nothing.
Indeed every living being is no more than a puff of wind,
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6: The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
What, then, can I hope for, Lord?
I put my hope in you.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
Save me from all my sins,
and don't let fools laugh at me.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
I will keep quiet, I will not say a word,
for you are the one who made me suffer like this.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
Don't punish me any more!
I am about to die from your blows.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
You punish our sins by your rebukes,
and like a moth you destroy what we love.
Indeed we are no more than a puff of wind!
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12:
Hear my prayer, 
LORD
,
and listen to my cry;
come to my aid when I weep.
Like all my ancestors
I am only your guest for a little while.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
13:
Leave me alone so that I may have some happiness
before I go away and am no more.
The Confession of a Sufferer#Ps 39 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
I waited patiently for the 
LORD
's help;
then he listened to me and heard my cry.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
He pulled me out of a dangerous pit,
out of the deadly quicksand.
He set me safely on a rock
and made me secure.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
He taught me to sing a new song,
a song of praise to our God.
Many who see this will take warning
and will put their trust in the 
LORD
.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
Happy are those who trust the 
LORD
,
who do not turn to idols
or join those who worship false gods.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
You have done many things for us, O 
LORD
 our God;
there is no one like you!
You have made many wonderful plans for us.
I could never speak of them all —
their number is so great!
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
You do not want sacrifices and offerings;
you do not ask for animals burnt whole on the altar
or for sacrifices to take away sins.
Instead, you have given me ears to hear you,
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
and so I answered, “Here I am;
your instructions for me are in the book of the Law.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 40.7 your instructions… Law; or my devotion to you is recorded in your book.
8:
How I love to do your will, my God!
I keep your teaching in my heart.”
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
In the assembly of all your people, 
LORD
,
I told the good news that you save us.
You know that I will never stop telling it.
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
I have not kept the news of salvation to myself;
I have always spoken of your faithfulness and help.
In the assembly of all your people I have not been silent
about your loyalty and constant love.
LORD
A Song of Praise#Ps 40 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
, I know you will never stop being merciful to me.
Your love and loyalty will always keep me safe.
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
12:
I am surrounded by many troubles —
too many to count!
My sins have caught up with me,
and I can no longer see;
they are more than the hairs of my head,
and I have lost my courage.
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
13:
Save me, 
LORD
! Help me now!
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
14:
May those who try to kill me
be completely defeated and confused.
May those who are happy because of my troubles
be turned back and disgraced.
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
15:
May those who jeer at me
be dismayed by their defeat.
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
16:
May all who come to you
be glad and joyful.
May all who are thankful for your salvation
always say, “How great is the 
LORD
!”
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70
17:
I am weak and poor, O Lord,
but you have not forgotten me.
You are my saviour and my God —
hurry to my aid!
A Prayer for Help
Ps 70

1:
Happy are those who are concerned for the poor;
the 
LORD
 will help them when they are in trouble.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2: A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
The 
LORD
 will help them when they are sick
and will restore them to health.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
I said, “I have sinned against you, 
LORD
;
be merciful to me and heal me.”
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
My enemies say cruel things about me.
They want me to die and be forgotten.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6: A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
All who hate me whisper to each other about me,
they imagine the worst about
 me.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 41.7 imagine the worst about; or make evil plans to harm.
8:
They say, “He is fatally ill;
he will never leave his bed again.”
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9:
Even my best friend, the one I trusted most,
the one who shared my food,
has turned against me.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
Be merciful to me, 
LORD
, and restore my health,
and I will pay my enemies back.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
They will not triumph over me,
and I will know that you are pleased with me.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12:
You will help me, because I do what is right;
you will keep me in your presence for ever.
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
13:
Praise the 
LORD
, the God of Israel!
Praise him now and for ever!
Amen! Amen!
A Prayer in Sickness#Ps 41 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
As a deer longs for a stream of cool water,
so I long for you, O God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
2:
I thirst for you, the living God;
when can I go and worship in your presence?
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
3:
Day and night I cry,
and tears are my only food;
all the time my enemies ask me,
“Where is your God?”
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
4:
My heart breaks when I remember the past,
when I went with the crowds to the house of God
and led them as they walked along,
a happy crowd, singing and shouting praise to God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
5:
Why am I so sad?
Why am I so troubled?
I will put my hope in God,
and once again I will praise him,
my saviour and my God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
6-7:
Here in exile my heart is breaking,
and so I turn my thoughts to him.
He has sent waves of sorrow over my soul;
chaos roars at me like a flood,
like waterfalls thundering down to the Jordan
from Mount Hermon and Mount Mizar.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
8:
May the 
LORD
 show his constant love during the day,
so that I may have a song at night,
a prayer to the God of my life.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
9:
To God, my defender, I say,
“Why have you forgotten me?
Why must I go on suffering
from the cruelty of my enemies?”
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
10:
I am crushed by their insults,
as they keep on asking me,
“Where is your God?”
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
11:
Why am I so sad?
Why am I so troubled?
I will put my hope in God,
and once again I will praise him,
my saviour and my God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile#Ps 42 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.

Continuation of Ps 42
2:
You are my protector;
why have you abandoned me?
Why must I go on suffering
from the cruelty of my enemies?
The Prayer of Someone in Exile
Continuation of Ps 42
3:
Send your light and your truth;
may they lead me
and bring me back to Zion, your sacred hill,
and to your Temple, where you live.
