Continuation of Ps 42
4:
Then I will go to your altar, O God;
you are the source of my happiness.
I will play my harp and sing praise to you,
O God, my God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile
Continuation of Ps 42
5:
Why am I so sad?
Why am I so troubled?
I will put my hope in God,
and once again I will praise him,
my saviour and my God.
The Prayer of Someone in Exile
Continuation of Ps 42

1:
With our own ears we have heard it, O God —
our ancestors have told us about it,
about the great things you did in their time,
in the days of long ago:
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
2:
how you yourself drove out the heathen
and established your people in their land;
how you punished the other nations
and caused your own to prosper.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
3: A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
4: A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
; 44.4 Some ancient translations and my God; you give; Hebrew O God; give.
5: and by your power we defeat our enemies.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
6:
I do not trust in my bow
or in my sword to save me;
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
7:
but you have saved us from our enemies
and defeated those who hate us.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
8:
We will always praise you
and give thanks to you for ever.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
9:
But now you have rejected us and let us be defeated;
you no longer march out with our armies.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
10:
You made us run from our enemies,
and they took for themselves what was ours.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
11:
You allowed us to be slaughtered like sheep;
you scattered us in foreign countries.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
12:
You sold your own people for a small price
as though they had little value.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
; 44.12 as… value; or and made no profit from the sale.
13:
Our neighbours see what you did to us,
and they mock us and laugh at us.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
14:
You have made us an object of contempt among the nations;
they shake their heads at us in scorn.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
15:
I am always in disgrace;
I am covered with shame
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
16:
from hearing the sneers and insults
of my enemies and those who hate me.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
17:
All this has happened to us,
even though we have not forgotten you
or broken the covenant you made with us.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
18:
We have not been disloyal to you;
we have not disobeyed your commands.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
19:
Yet you left us helpless among wild animals;
you abandoned us in deepest darkness.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
20:
If we had stopped worshipping our God
and prayed to a foreign god,
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
21:
you would surely have discovered it,
because you know our secret thoughts.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
22:
But it is on your account that we are being killed all the time,
that we are treated like sheep to be slaughtered.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
23:
Wake up, Lord! Why are you asleep?
Rouse yourself! Don't reject us for ever!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
24:
Why are you hiding from us?
Don't forget our suffering and trouble!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
25:
We fall crushed to the ground;
we lie defeated in the dust.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.
26:
Come to our aid!
Because of your constant love save us!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 44 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah.

1:
Beautiful words fill my mind,
as I compose this song for the king.
Like the pen of a good writer
my tongue is ready with a poem.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
2: A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
3:
Buckle on your sword, mighty king;
you are glorious and majestic.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
4:
Ride on in majesty to victory
for the defence of truth and justice!
Your strength will win you great victories!
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
; 45.4 Probable text and justice; Hebrew and meekness of justice.
5: A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
6: A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
; 45.6 The kingdom that God has given you; or Your kingdom, O God; or Your divine kingdom.; Heb 1.8–9
7:
you love what is right and hate what is evil.
That is why God, your God, has chosen you
and has poured out more happiness on you
than on any other king.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
8:
The perfume of myrrh and aloes is on your clothes;
musicians entertain you in palaces decorated with ivory.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
9:
Among the ladies of your court are daughters of kings,
and on the right of your throne stands the queen,
wearing ornaments of finest gold.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
10:
Bride of the king, listen to what I say —
forget your people and your relatives.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
11:
Your beauty will make the king desire you;
he is your master, so you must obey him.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
12:
The people of Tyre will bring you gifts;
rich people will try to win your favour.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
13:
The princess is in the palace — how beautiful she is!
Her gown is made of gold thread.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
14:
In her colourful gown she is led to the king,
followed by her bridesmaids,
and they also are brought to him.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
15:
With joy and gladness they come
and enter the king's palace.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
16:
You, my king, will have many sons
to succeed your ancestors as kings,
and you will make them rulers over the whole earth.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.
17:
My song will keep your fame alive for ever,
and everyone will praise you for all time to come.
A Royal Wedding Song#Ps 45 Hebrew title: A poem by the clan of Korah; a love song.

1:
God is our shelter and strength,

So we will not be afraid, even if the earth is shaken

3:
even if the seas roar and rage,
and the hills are shaken by the violence.
God is with Us#Ps 46 Hebrew title: A song by the clan of Korah.
4:
There is a river that brings joy to the city of God,
to the sacred house of the Most High.
God is with Us#Ps 46 Hebrew title: A song by the clan of Korah.
5:
God is in that city, and it will never be destroyed;

God is with Us#Ps 46 Hebrew title: A song by the clan of Korah.
6:
Nations are terrified, kingdoms are shaken;

The 
LORD
 Almighty is with us;
the God of Jacob is our refuge.

 has done.

11:
The

 Almighty is with us;
the God of Jacob is our refuge.

, the Most High, is to be feared;

LORD
 goes up.

 of the God of Abraham.

 is great and is to be highly praised

1:

2:
great and small alike,

3:
My thoughts will be clear;

The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.
4:
I will turn my attention to proverbs
and explain their meaning as I play the harp.
The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.
5:
I am not afraid in times of danger
when I am surrounded by enemies,
The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.
6:
by evil people who trust in their riches
and boast of their great wealth.
The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.

The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.

because the payment for a human life is too great.
What he could pay would never be enough
The Foolishness of Trusting in Riches#Ps 49 Hebrew title: A psalm by the clan of Korah.

to keep him from the grave,

 are their homes for ever;

; 49.11 Some ancient translations graves; Hebrew inner thoughts.

1:
The Almighty God, the 
LORD
, speaks;
he calls to the whole earth from east to west.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
2:
God shines from Zion,
the city perfect in its beauty.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
3: True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
4:
He calls heaven and earth as witnesses
to see him judge his people.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
5:
He says, “Gather my faithful people to me,
those who made a covenant with me by offering a sacrifice.”
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
6:
The heavens proclaim that God is righteous,
that he himself is judge.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
7: True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
8:
I do not reprimand you because of your sacrifices
and the burnt offerings you always bring me.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
9:
And yet I do not need bulls from your farms
or goats from your flocks;
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
10:
all the animals in the forest are mine
and the cattle on thousands of hills.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
11:
All the wild birds are mine
and all living things in the fields.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
12:
“If I were hungry, I would not ask you for food,
for the world and everything in it is mine.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
13:
Do I eat the flesh of bulls
or drink the blood of goats?
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
14:
Let the giving of thanks be your sacrifice to God,
and give the Almighty all that you promised.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
; 50.14 Let the giving… to God; or Offer your thanksgiving sacrifice to God.
15: True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.

But God says to the wicked,
“Why should you recite my commandments?
Why should you talk about my covenant?
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
17:
You refuse to let me correct you;
you reject my commands.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
18:
You become the friend of every thief you see
and you associate with adulterers.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
19:
“You are always ready to speak evil;
you never hesitate to tell lies.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
20:
You are ready to accuse your own relatives
and to find fault with them.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
21:
You have done all this, and I have said nothing,
so you thought that I was like you.
But now I reprimand you
and make the matter plain to you.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
22:
“Listen to this, you that ignore me,
or I will destroy you,
and there will be no one to save you.
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
23:
Giving thanks is the sacrifice that honours me,
and I will surely save all who obey me.”
True Worship#Ps 50 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
