A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
2:
Hear my prayer, O God;
listen to my words!
A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
3: A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
4:
But God is my helper.
The Lord is my defender.
A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
5:
May God use their own evil to punish my enemies.
He will destroy them because he is faithful.
A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
6:
I will gladly offer you a sacrifice, O 
LORD
;
I will give you thanks
because you are good.
A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1
7:
You have rescued me from all my troubles,
and I have seen my enemies defeated.
A Prayer for Protection from Enemies#Ps 54 Hebrew title: A poem by David, after the men from Ziph went to Saul and told him that David was hiding in their territory.#1 Sam 23.19; 26.1

1:
Hear my prayer, O God;
don't turn away from my plea!
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
2:
Listen to me and answer me;
I am worn out by my worries.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
3:
I am terrified by the threats of my enemies,
crushed by the oppression of the wicked.
They bring trouble on me;
they are angry with me and hate me.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
4:
I am terrified,
and the terrors of death crush me.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
5:
I am gripped by fear and trembling;
I am overcome with horror.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
6:
I wish I had wings, like a dove.
I would fly away and find rest.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
7:
I would fly far away
and make my home in the desert.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
8:
I would quickly find myself a shelter
from the raging wind and the storm.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
9:
Confuse the speech of my enemies, O Lord!
I see violence and riots in the city,
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
10:
surrounding it day and night,
filling it with crime and trouble.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
11:
There is destruction everywhere;
the streets are full of oppression and fraud.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
12: The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
13:
But it is you, my companion,
my colleague and close friend.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
14:
We had intimate talks with each other
and worshipped together in the Temple.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
15: The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
16:
But I call to the 
LORD
 God for help,
and he will save me.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
17:
Morning, noon, and night
my complaints and groans go up to him,
and he will hear my voice.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
18:
He will bring me safely back
from the battles that I fight
against so many enemies.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
19: The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
20:
My former companion attacked his friends;
he broke his promises.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
21:
His words were smoother than cream,
but there was hatred in his heart;
his words were as soothing as oil,
but they cut like sharp swords.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
22:
Leave your troubles with the 
LORD
,
and he will defend you;
he never lets honest people be defeated.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.
23:
But you, O God, will bring those murderers and liars to their graves
before half their life is over.
As for me, I will trust in you.
The Prayer of Someone Betrayed by a Friend#Ps 55 Hebrew title: A poem by David.

1: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
2:
All day long my opponents attack me.
There are so many who fight against me.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
3:
When I am afraid, O 
LORD
 Almighty,
I put my trust in you.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
4: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
5:
My enemies make trouble for me all day long;
they are always planning how to hurt me!
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
6:
They gather in hiding places
and watch everything I do,
hoping to kill me.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
7: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
; 56.7 Probable text Punish; Hebrew Save.
8: A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
9:
The day I call to you,
my enemies will be turned back.
I know this: God
 is on my side —
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
; 56.9 I know this: God; or Because I know that God.
10:
the 
LORD
, whose promises I praise.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
11:
In him I trust, and I will not be afraid.
What can a mere human being do to me?
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
12:
O God, I will offer you what I have promised;
I will give you my offering of thanksgiving,
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15
13:
because you have rescued me from death
and kept me from defeat.
And so I walk in the presence of God,
in the light that shines on the living.
A Prayer of Trust in God#Ps 56 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after the Philistines captured him in Gath.#1 Sam 21.13–15

1:
Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful,
because I come to you for safety.
In the shadow of your wings I find protection
until the raging storms are over.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
2:
I call to God, the Most High,
to God, who supplies my every need.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
3: A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
4:
I am surrounded by enemies,
who are like lions hungry for human flesh.
Their teeth are like spears and arrows;
their tongues are like sharp swords.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
5:
Show your greatness in the sky, O God,
and your glory over all the earth.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
6:
My enemies have spread a net to catch me;
I am overcome with distress.
They dug a pit in my path,
but fell into it themselves.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
7:
I have complete confidence, O God;
I will sing and praise you!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
8:
Wake up, my soul!
Wake up, my harp and lyre!
I will wake up the sun.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
9:
I will thank you, O Lord, among the nations.
I will praise you among the peoples.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
10:
Your constant love reaches the heavens;
your faithfulness touches the skies.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3
11:
Show your greatness in the sky, O God,
and your glory over all the earth.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 57 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after he fled from Saul in the cave.#1 Sam 22.1; 24.3

1:
Do you rulers
 ever give a just decision?
Do you judge everyone fairly?
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 58.1 rulers; or gods.
2:
No! You think only of the evil you can do,
and commit crimes of violence in the land.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
Evildoers go wrong all their lives;
they tell lies from the day they are born.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
They are full of poison like snakes;
they stop up their ears like a deaf cobra,
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
which does not hear the voice of the snake charmer,
or the chant of the clever magician.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6: Break the teeth of these fierce lions, O God.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
May they disappear like water draining away;
may they be crushed like weeds on a path.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 58.7 Probable text may… path; Hebrew unclear.
8:
May they be like snails that dissolve into slime;
may they be like a baby born dead that never sees the light.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9: A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 58.9 Verse 9 in Hebrew is unclear.
10:
The righteous will be glad when they see sinners punished;
they will wade through the blood of the wicked.
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
People will say, “The righteous are indeed rewarded;
there is indeed a God who judges the world.”
A Prayer for God to Punish the Wicked#Ps 58 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
Save me from my enemies, my God;
protect me from those who attack me!
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
2:
Save me from those evil people;
rescue me from those murderers!
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
3: A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
4:
nor because of any fault of mine, O 
LORD
,
that they hurry to their places.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
5: A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
6:
They come back in the evening,
snarling like dogs as they go about the city.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
7:
Listen to their insults and threats.
Their tongues are like swords in their mouths,
yet they think that no one hears them.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
8:
But you laugh at them, 
LORD
;
you mock all the heathen.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
9:
I have confidence in your strength;
you are my refuge, O God.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
10:
My God loves me and will come to me;
he will let me see my enemies defeated.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
11:
Do not kill them, O God, or my people may forget.
Scatter them by your strength and defeat them,
O Lord, our protector.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
12: A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
13:
destroy them in your anger;
destroy them completely.
Then everyone will know that God rules in Israel,
that his rule extends over all the earth.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
14:
My enemies come back in the evening,
snarling like dogs as they go about the city,
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
15:
like dogs roaming about for food
and growling if they do not find enough.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
16:
But I will sing about your strength;
every morning I will sing aloud of your constant love.
You have been a refuge for me,
a shelter in my time of trouble.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11
17:
I will praise you, my defender.
My refuge is God,
the God who loves me.
A Prayer for Safety#Ps 59 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, after Saul sent men to watch his house in order to kill him.#1 Sam 19.11

1:
You have rejected us, God, and defeated us;
you have been angry with us — but now turn back to us.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
; 60.1 angry with us… us; or angry with us and turned your back on us.
2:
You have made the land tremble, and you have cut it open;
now heal its wounds, because it is falling apart.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
3:
You have made your people suffer greatly;
we stagger around as though we were drunk.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
4:
You have warned those who show you reverence,
so that they might escape destruction.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
5:
Save us by your might; answer our prayer,
so that the people you love may be rescued.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
6:
From his sanctuary
 God has said,
“In triumph I will divide Shechem
and distribute the Valley of Sukkoth to my people.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
; 60.6 From his sanctuary; or In his holiness.
7: A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
8:
But I will use Moab as my washbasin
and I will throw my sandals on Edom,
as a sign that I own it.
Did the Philistines think they would shout in triumph over me?”

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
9:
Who, O God, will take me into the fortified city?
Who will lead me to Edom?

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
10:
Have you really rejected us?
Aren't you going to march out with our armies?

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
11:
Help us against the enemy;
human help is worthless.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12
12:
With God on our side we will win;
he will defeat our enemies.

A Prayer for Deliverance#Ps 60 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, for teaching, when he fought against the Arameans from Naharaim and from Zobah, and Joab turned back and killed 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.#2 Sam 8.13; 1 Chr 18.12

1:
Hear my cry, O God;
listen to my prayer!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
In despair and far from home
I call to you!
Take me to a safe refuge,
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
for you are my protector,
my strong defence against my enemies.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
Let me live in your sanctuary all my life;
let me find safety under your wings.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
You have heard my promises, O God,
and you have given me what belongs to those who honour you.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
Add many years to the king's life;
let him live on and on!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
May he rule for ever in your presence, O God;
protect him with your constant love and faithfulness.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
So I will always sing praises to you,
as I offer you daily what I have promised.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 61 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
I wait patiently for God to save me;
I depend on him alone.
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2: Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
How much longer will all of you attack someone
who is no stronger than a broken-down fence?
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
You only want to bring him down from his place of honour;
you take pleasure in lies.
You speak words of blessing,
but in your heart you curse him.
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5:
I depend on God alone;
I put my hope in him.
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6: Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7: Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
Trust in God at all times, my people.
Tell him all your troubles,
for he is our refuge.
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
9: Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10: Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
11:
More than once I have heard God say
that power belongs to him
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
12:
and that his love is constant.
You yourself, O Lord, reward everyone according to his deeds.
Confidence in God's Protection#Ps 62 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
O God, you are my God,
and I long for you.
My whole being desires you;
like a dry, worn-out, and waterless land,
my soul is thirsty for you.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
2:
Let me see you in the sanctuary;
let me see how mighty and glorious you are.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
3:
Your constant love is better than life itself,
and so I will praise you.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
4:
I will give you thanks as long as I live;
I will raise my hands to you in prayer.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
5:
My soul will feast and be satisfied,
and I will sing glad songs of praise to you.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
6:
As I lie in bed, I remember you;
all night long I think of you,
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
7:
because you have always been my help.
In the shadow of your wings I sing for joy.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
8:
I cling to you,
and your hand keeps me safe.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
9:
Those who are trying to kill me
will go down into the world of the dead.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
10:
They will be killed in battle,
and their bodies eaten by wolves.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14
11:
Because God gives him victory,
the king will rejoice.
Those who make promises in God's name will praise him,
but the mouths of liars will be shut.
Longing for God#Ps 63 Hebrew title: A psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah.#1 Sam 23.14

1:
I am in trouble, God — listen to my prayer!
I am afraid of my enemies — save my life!
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
2:
Protect me from the plots of the wicked,
from evil mobs.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
3:
They sharpen their tongues like swords
and aim cruel words like arrows.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
4:
They are quick to spread their shameless lies;
they destroy good people with cowardly slander.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
5: A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
6:
They make evil plans and say,
“We have planned a perfect crime.”
The human heart and mind are a mystery.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
7:
But God shoots his arrows at them,
and suddenly they are wounded.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
8:
He will destroy them because of those words;
all who see them will shake their heads.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
; 64.8 Probable text He will destroy them because of those words; Hebrew They will destroy him, those words are against them.
9: A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.
10:
All righteous people will rejoice
because of what the 
LORD
 has done.
They will find safety in him;
all good people will praise him.
A Prayer for Protection#Ps 64 Hebrew title: A psalm by David.

1:
O God, it is right for us to praise you in Zion
and keep our promises to you,
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
2:
because you answer prayers.
People everywhere will come to you
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
3:
on account of their sins.
Our faults defeat us,
but you forgive them.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 65.3 One ancient translation us; Hebrew me.
4:
Happy are those whom you choose,
whom you bring to live in your sanctuary.
We shall be satisfied with the good things of your house,
the blessings of your sacred Temple.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
5:
You answer us by giving us victory
and you do wonderful things to save us.
People all over the world
and across the distant seas trust in you.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
6:
You set the mountains in place by your strength,
showing your mighty power.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
7: Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
8:
The whole world stands in awe
of the great things that you have done.
Your deeds bring shouts of joy
from one end of the earth to the other.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
9: Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
10:
you send abundant rain on the ploughed fields
and soak them with water;
you soften the soil with showers
and cause the young plants to grow.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
11:
What a rich harvest your goodness provides!
Wherever you go there is plenty.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
12:
The pastures are filled with flocks;
the hillsides are full of joy.
Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
13: Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 65 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.

1: Praise God with shouts of joy, all people!
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
2:
Sing to the glory of his name;
offer him glorious praise!
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
3:
Say to God, “How wonderful are the things you do!
Your power is so great
that your enemies bow down in fear before you.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
4: A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
5:
Come and see what God has done,
his wonderful acts for mankind.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
6: A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
7:
He rules for ever by his might
and keeps his eyes on the nations.
Let no rebels rise against him.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
8:
Praise our God, all nations;
let your praise be heard.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
9:
He has kept us alive
and has not allowed us to fall.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
10: A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
11:
You let us fall into a trap
and placed heavy burdens on our backs.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
12: A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
; 66.12 Some ancient translations safety; Hebrew overflowing.
13:
I will bring burnt offerings to your house;
I will offer you what I promised.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
14:
I will give you what I said I would
when I was in trouble.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
15: A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
16:
Come and listen, all who honour God,
and I will tell you what he has done for me.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
17:
I cried to him for help;
I praised him with songs.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
18:
If I had ignored my sins,
the Lord would not have listened to me.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
19:
But God has indeed heard me;
he has listened to my prayer.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.
20:
I praise God,
because he did not reject my prayer
or keep back his constant love from me.
A Song of Praise and Thanksgiving#Ps 66 Hebrew title: A song.

1:
God, be merciful to us and bless us;
look on us with kindness,
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
2:
so that the whole world may know your will;
so that all nations may know your salvation.
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
3:
May the peoples praise you, O God;
may all the peoples praise you!
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
4:
May the nations be glad and sing for joy,
because you judge the peoples with justice
and guide every nation on earth.
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
5:
May the peoples praise you, O God;
may all the peoples praise you!
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
6:
The land has produced its harvest;
God, our God, has blessed us.
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.
7:
God has blessed us;
may all people everywhere honour him.
A Song of Thanksgiving#Ps 67 Hebrew title: A psalm; a song.

1:
God rises up and scatters his enemies.
Those who hate him run away in defeat.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
2: A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
3:
But the righteous are glad and rejoice in his presence;
they are happy and shout for joy.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
4:
Sing to God, sing praises to his name;
prepare a way for him who rides on the clouds.
His name is the 
LORD
 — be glad in his presence!
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 68.4 on the clouds; or across the desert.
5:
God, who lives in his sacred Temple,
cares for orphans and protects widows.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
6:
He gives the lonely a home to live in
and leads prisoners out into happy freedom,
but rebels will have to live in a desolate land.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
7:
O God, when you led your people,
when you marched across the desert,
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
8:
the earth shook, and the sky poured down rain,
because of the coming of the God of Sinai,
the coming of the God of Israel.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 68.8 God of Sinai: As the people of Israel went from Egypt to Canaan, God revealed himself to them at Mount Sinai (see Ex 19.16–25).; Ex 19.18
9:
You caused abundant rain to fall
and restored your worn-out land;
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
10:
your people made their home there;
in your goodness you provided for the poor.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
11:
The Lord gave the command,
and many women carried the news:
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
12:
“Kings and their armies are running away!”
The women at home divided what was captured:
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
13:
figures of doves covered with silver,
whose wings glittered with fine gold.
(Why did some of you stay among the sheep pens on the day of battle?)
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
14:
When Almighty God scattered the kings on Mount Zalmon,
he caused snow to fall there.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
15:
What a mighty mountain is Bashan,
a mountain of many peaks!
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
16:
Why from your mighty peaks do you look with scorn
on the mountain
 on which God chose to live?
The 
LORD
 will live there for ever!
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 68.16 mountain: See 2.6.
17:
With his many thousands of mighty chariots
the Lord comes from Sinai
 into the holy place.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 68.17 Probable text comes from Sinai; Hebrew in them, Sinai.
18:
He goes up to the heights,
taking many captives with him;
he receives gifts from rebellious people.
The 
LORD
 God will live there.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; Eph 4.8
19: A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
20:
Our God is a God who saves;
he is the 
LORD
, our Lord,
who rescues us from death.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
21:
God will surely break the heads of his enemies,
of those who persist in their sinful ways.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
22:
The Lord has said, “I will bring your enemies back from Bashan;
I will bring them back from the depths of the ocean,
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
23:
so that you may wade in their blood,
and your dogs may lap up as much as they want.”
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
24:
O God, your march of triumph is seen by all,
the procession of God, my king, into his sanctuary.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
25:
The singers are in front, the musicians are behind,
in between are the young women beating the tambourines.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
26:
“Praise God in the meeting of his people;
praise the 
LORD
, all you descendants of Jacob!”
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
27:
First comes Benjamin, the smallest tribe,
then the leaders of Judah with their group,
followed by the leaders of Zebulun and Naphtali.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
28:
Show your power, O God,
the power you have used on our behalf
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
29:
from your Temple in Jerusalem,
where kings bring gifts to you.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
30:
Rebuke Egypt, that wild animal in the reeds;
rebuke the nations, that herd of bulls with their calves,
until they all bow down and offer you their silver.
Scatter those people who love to make war!
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
; 68.30 Verse 30 in Hebrew is unclear.
31:
Ambassadors
 will come from Egypt;
the Ethiopians
 will raise their hands in prayer to God.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
68.31 Ethiopians: See Word List.; 68.31 Some ancient translations Ambassadors; Hebrew unclear.
32:
Sing to God, kingdoms of the world,
sing praise to the Lord,
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
33:
to him who rides in the sky,
the ancient sky.
Listen to him shout with a mighty roar.
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
34: A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.
35:
How awesome is God as he comes from his sanctuary —
the God of Israel!
He gives strength and power to his people.
Praise God!
A National Song of Triumph#Ps 68 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a song.

1:
Save me, O God!
The water is up to my neck;
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
2:
I am sinking in deep mud,
and there is no solid ground;
I am out in deep water,
and the waves are about to drown me.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
3:
I am worn out from calling for help,
and my throat is aching.
I have strained my eyes,
looking for your help.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
4:
Those who hate me for no reason
are more numerous than the hairs of my head.
My enemies tell lies against me;
they are strong and want to kill me.
They made me give back things I did not steal.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
5:
My sins, O God, are not hidden from you;
you know how foolish I have been.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
6:
Don't let me bring shame on those who trust in you,
Sovereign 
LORD
 Almighty!
Don't let me bring disgrace to those who worship you,
O God of Israel!
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
7:
It is for your sake that I have been insulted
and that I am covered with shame.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
8:
I am like a stranger to my relatives,
like a foreigner to my family.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
9:
My devotion to your Temple burns in me like a fire;
the insults which are hurled at you fall on me.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
10: A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
69.10 Some ancient translations humble myself; Hebrew cry.;
11:
I dress myself in clothes of mourning,
and they laugh at me.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
12:
They talk about me in the streets,
and drunkards make up songs about me.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
13:
But as for me, I will pray to you, 
LORD
;
answer me, God, at a time you choose.
Answer me because of your great love,
because you keep your promise to save.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
14: A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
15: A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
16:
Answer me, 
LORD
, in the goodness of your constant love;
in your great compassion turn to me!
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
17:
Don't hide yourself from your servant;
I am in great trouble — answer me now!
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
18:
Come to me and save me;
rescue me from my enemies.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
19: A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
20:
Insults have broken my heart,
and I am in despair.
I had hoped for sympathy, but there was none;
for comfort, but I found none.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
21:
When I was hungry, they gave me poison;
when I was thirsty, they offered me vinegar.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
22:
May their banquets cause their ruin;
may their sacred feasts cause their downfall.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
23:
Strike them with blindness!
Make their backs always weak!
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
24:
Pour out your anger on them;
let your indignation overtake them.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
25:
May their camps be left deserted;
may no one be left alive in their tents.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
; Acts 1.20
26:
They persecute those whom you have punished;
they talk about the sufferings of those you have wounded.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
27:
Keep a record of all their sins;
don't let them have any part in your salvation.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
28:
May their names be erased from the book of the living;
may they not be included in the list of your people.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
29:
But I am in pain and despair;
lift me up, O God, and save me!
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
30:
I will praise God with a song;
I will proclaim his greatness by giving him thanks.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
31:
This will please the 
LORD
 more than offering him cattle,
more than sacrificing a full-grown bull.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
32:
When the oppressed see this, they will be glad;
those who worship God will be encouraged.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
33:
The 
LORD
 listens to those in need
and does not forget his people in prison.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
34:
Praise God, O heaven and earth,
seas and all creatures in them.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
35: A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.
36:
the descendants of his servants will inherit it,
and those who love him will live there.
A Cry for Help#Ps 69 Hebrew title: By David.

1:
Save me, O God!
LORD
, help me now!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 70 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
2:
May those who try to kill me
be defeated and confused.
May those who are happy because of my troubles
be turned back and disgraced.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 70 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
3:
May those who jeer at me
be dismayed by their defeat.
A Prayer for Help#Ps 70 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
4:
May all who come to you
be glad and joyful.
May all who are thankful for your salvation
always say, “How great is God!”
A Prayer for Help#Ps 70 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.
5:
I am weak and poor;
come to me quickly, O God.
You are my saviour, O 
LORD
 —
hurry to my aid!
A Prayer for Help#Ps 70 Hebrew title: A psalm by David; a lament.

1: An Old Person's Prayer
2:
Because you are righteous, help me and rescue me.
Listen to me and save me!
An Old Person's Prayer
3:
Be my secure shelter
and a strong fortress
 to protect me;
you are my refuge and defence.
An Old Person's Prayer
; 71.3 One ancient translation a strong fortress; Hebrew to go always you commanded.
4:
My God, rescue me from the wicked,
from the power of those who are cruel and evil.
An Old Person's Prayer
5:
Sovereign 
LORD
, I put my hope in you;
I have trusted in you since I was young.
An Old Person's Prayer
6:
I have relied on you all my life;
you have protected
 me since the day I was born.
I will always praise you.
An Old Person's Prayer
; 71.6 Some ancient translations protected; Hebrew unclear.
7:
My life has been an example to many,
because you have been my strong defender.
An Old Person's Prayer
8:
All day long I praise you
and proclaim your glory.
An Old Person's Prayer
9:
Do not reject me now that I am old;
do not abandon me now that I am feeble.
An Old Person's Prayer
10:
My enemies want to kill me;
they talk and plot against me.
An Old Person's Prayer
11: An Old Person's Prayer
12:
Don't stay so far away, O God;
my God, hurry to my aid!
An Old Person's Prayer
13:
May those who attack me
be defeated and destroyed.
May those who try to hurt me
be shamed and disgraced.
An Old Person's Prayer
14:
I will always put my hope in you;
I will praise you more and more.
An Old Person's Prayer
15: An Old Person's Prayer
16:
I will go in the strength of the 
LORD
 God;
I will proclaim your goodness, yours alone.
An Old Person's Prayer
17:
You have taught me ever since I was young,
and I still tell of your wonderful acts.
An Old Person's Prayer
18:
Now that I am old and my hair is grey,
do not abandon me, O God!
Be with me while I proclaim your power and might
to all generations to come.
An Old Person's Prayer
19: An Old Person's Prayer
20: An Old Person's Prayer
21:
You will make me greater than ever;
you will comfort me again.
An Old Person's Prayer
22:
I will indeed praise you with the harp;
I will praise your faithfulness, my God.
On my harp I will play hymns to you,
the Holy One of Israel.
An Old Person's Prayer
23: An Old Person's Prayer
24:
I will speak of your righteousness all day long,
because those who tried to harm me
have been defeated and disgraced.
An Old Person's Prayer

1:
Teach the king to judge with your righteousness, O God;
share with him your own justice,
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
2:
so that he will rule over your people with justice
and govern the oppressed with righteousness.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
3:
May the land enjoy prosperity;
may it experience righteousness.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
4: A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
5:
May your people worship you as long as the sun shines,
as long as the moon gives light, for ages to come.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
6:
May the king be like rain on the fields,
like showers falling on the land.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
7:
May righteousness flourish in his lifetime,
and may prosperity last as long as the moon gives light.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
8:
His kingdom will reach from sea to sea,
from the Euphrates to the ends of the earth.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
; Zech 9.10
9:
The peoples of the desert will bow down before him;
his enemies will throw themselves to the ground.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
10: A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
; 72.10; 72.15 Sheba and Seba: Sheba was towards the south in Arabia and Seba was on the opposite side of the Red Sea.
11:
All kings will bow down before him;
all nations will serve him.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
12:
He rescues the poor who call to him,
and those who are needy and neglected.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
13:
He has pity on the weak and poor;
he saves the lives of those in need.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
14:
He rescues them from oppression and violence;
their lives are precious to him.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
15: A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
; 72.15; 72.10 Sheba and Seba: Sheba was towards the south in Arabia and Seba was on the opposite side of the Red Sea.
16:
May there be plenty of corn in the land;
may the hills be covered with crops,
as fruitful as those of Lebanon.
May the cities be filled with people,
like fields full of grass.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
17:
May the king's name never be forgotten;
may his fame last as long as the sun.
May all nations ask God to bless them
as he has blessed the king.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
; 72.17 as he… king; or and may they wish happiness for the king.
18:
Praise the 
LORD
, the God of Israel!
He alone does these wonderful things.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
19:
Praise his glorious name for ever!
May his glory fill the whole world.
Amen! Amen!
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.
20: This is the end of the prayers of David son of Jesse.
A Prayer for the King#Ps 72 Hebrew title: By Solomon.

1:
God is indeed good to Israel,
to those who have pure hearts.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
2:
But I had nearly lost confidence;
my faith was almost gone
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
3:
because I was jealous of the proud
when I saw that things go well for the wicked.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
4:
They do not suffer pain;
they are strong and healthy.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
5:
They do not suffer as other people do;
they do not have the troubles that others have.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
6:
And so they wear pride like a necklace
and violence like a robe;
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
7:
their hearts pour out evil,
and their minds are busy with wicked schemes.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
; 73.7 Some ancient translations their hearts pour out evil; Hebrew unclear.
8:
They laugh at other people and speak of evil things;
they are proud and make plans to oppress others.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
9:
They speak evil of God in heaven
and give arrogant orders to everyone on earth,
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
10:
so that even God's people turn to them
and eagerly believe whatever they say.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
; 73.10 Verse 10 in Hebrew is unclear.
11:
They say, “God will not know;
the Most High will not find out.”
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
12:
That is what the wicked are like.
They have plenty and are always getting more.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
13:
Is it for nothing, then, that I have kept myself pure
and have not committed sin?
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
14:
O God, you have made me suffer all day long;
every morning you have punished me.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
15:
If I had said such things,
I would not be acting as one of your people.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
16:
I tried to think this problem through,
but it was too difficult for me
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
17:
until I went into your Temple.
Then I understood what will happen to the wicked.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
18:
You will put them in slippery places
and make them fall to destruction!
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
19:
They are instantly destroyed;
they go down to a horrible end.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
20:
They are like a dream that goes away in the morning;
when you rouse yourself, O Lord, they disappear.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
21:
When my thoughts were bitter
and my feelings were hurt,
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
22:
I was as stupid as an animal;
I did not understand you.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
23:
Yet I always stay close to you,
and you hold me by the hand.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
24:
You guide me with your instruction
and at the end you will receive me with honour.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
25:
What else have I in heaven but you?
Since I have you, what else could I want on earth?
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
26: The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
27:
Those who abandon you will certainly perish;
you will destroy those who are unfaithful to you.
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
28:
But as for me, how wonderful to be near God,
to find protection with the Sovereign 
LORD
and to proclaim all that he has done!
The Justice of God#Ps 73 Hebrew title: By Asaph.

1:
Why have you abandoned us like this, O God?
Will you be angry with your own people for ever?
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
2:
Remember your people, whom you chose for yourself long ago,
whom you brought out of slavery to be your own tribe.
Remember Mount Zion, where once you lived.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
3:
Walk over these total ruins;
our enemies have destroyed everything in the Temple.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
4:
Your enemies have shouted in triumph in your Temple;
they have placed their flags there as signs of victory.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
5:
They looked like woodmen
cutting down trees with their axes.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
; 74.5 Verse 5 in Hebrew is unclear.
6:
They smashed all the wooden panels
with their axes and sledgehammers.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
7:
They wrecked your Temple and set it on fire;
they desecrated the place where you are worshipped.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
8:
They wanted to crush us completely;
they burnt down every holy place in the land.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
9: A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
10:
How long, O God, will our enemies laugh at you?
Will they insult your name for ever?
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
11:
Why have you refused to help us?
Why do you keep your hands behind you?
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
; 74.11 Probable text Why do you keep your hands behind you; Hebrew unclear.
12:
But you have been our king from the beginning, O God;
you have saved us many times.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
13:
With your mighty strength you divided the sea
and smashed the heads of the sea monsters;
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
14:
you crushed the heads of the monster Leviathan
and fed his body to desert animals.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
74.14 animals; or people.; Job 41.1; Ps 104.26; Is 27.1; 74.14 Leviathan: A legendary monster which was a symbol of the forces of chaos and evil.
15:
You made springs and fountains flow;
you dried up large rivers.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
16:
You created the day and the night;
you set the sun and the moon in their places;
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
17:
you set the limits of the earth;
you made summer and winter.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
18:
But remember, O 
LORD
, that your enemies laugh at you,
that they are godless and despise you.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
19:
Don't abandon your helpless people to their cruel enemies;
don't forget your persecuted people!
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
20:
Remember the covenant you made with us.
There is violence in every dark corner of the land.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
21:
Don't let the oppressed be put to shame;
let those poor and needy people praise you.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
22:
Rouse yourself, God, and defend your cause!
Remember that godless people laugh at you all day long.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
23:
Don't forget the angry shouts of your enemies,
the continuous noise made by your foes.
A Prayer for National Deliverance#Ps 74 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.

1:
We give thanks to you, O God, we give thanks to you!
We proclaim how great you are
and tell of
 the wonderful things you have done.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
; 75.1 Some ancient translations We proclaim how great you are and tell of; Hebrew Your name is near and they tell of.
2:
“I have set a time for judgement,” says God,
“and I will judge with fairness.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
3:
Though every living creature tremble
and the earth itself be shaken,
I will keep its foundations firm.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
4: I tell the wicked not to be arrogant;
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
5: I tell them to stop their boasting.”
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
6:
Judgement does not come from the east or from the west,
from the north or from the south;
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
; 75.6 Probable text from the north or from the south; Hebrew from the wilderness of the mountains.
7:
it is God who is the judge,
condemning some and acquitting others.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
8:
The 
LORD
 holds a cup in his hand,
filled with the strong wine of his anger.
He pours it out, and all the wicked drink it;
they drink it down to the last drop.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
9:
But I will never stop speaking of the God of Jacob
or singing praises to him.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
10:
He will break the power of the wicked,
but the power of the righteous will be increased.
God the Judge#Ps 75 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.

1:
God is known in Judah;
his name is honoured in Israel.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
2:
He has his home in Jerusalem;
he lives on Mount Zion.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
3:
There he broke the arrows of the enemy,
their shields and swords, yes, all their weapons.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
4:
How glorious you are, O God!
How majestic, as you return from the mountains
where you defeated your foes.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
5: God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
6:
When you threatened them, O God of Jacob,
the horses and their riders fell dead.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
7:
But you, 
LORD
, are feared by all.
No one can stand in your presence
when you are angry.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
8:
You made your judgement known from heaven;
the world was afraid and kept silent,
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
9:
when you rose up to pronounce judgement,
to save all the oppressed on earth.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
10:
Human anger only results in more praise for you;
those who survive the wars will keep your festivals.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
; 76.10 One ancient translation will keep your festivals; verse 10 in Hebrew is unclear.
11: God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.
12:
he humbles proud princes
and terrifies great kings.
God the Victor#Ps 76 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a song.

1:
I cry aloud to God;
I cry aloud, and he hears me.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
2: Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
3:
When I think of God, I sigh;
when I meditate, I feel discouraged.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
4:
He keeps me awake all night;
I am so worried that I cannot speak.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
5:
I think of days gone by
and remember years of long ago.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
6:
I spend the night in deep thought;
I meditate, and this is what I ask myself:
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
; 77.6 Some ancient translations deep thought; Hebrew song.
7:
“Will the Lord always reject us?
Will he never again be pleased with us?
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
8:
Has he stopped loving us?
Does his promise no longer stand?
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
9:
Has God forgotten to be merciful?
Has anger taken the place of his compassion?”
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
10:
Then I said, “What hurts me most is this —
that God is no longer powerful.”
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
; 77.10 Verse 10 in Hebrew is unclear.
11:
I will remember your great deeds, 
LORD
;
I will recall the wonders you did in the past.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
12:
I will think about all that you have done;
I will meditate on all your mighty acts.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
13:
Everything you do, O God, is holy.
No god is as great as you.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
14:
You are the God who works miracles;
you showed your might among the nations.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
15:
By your power you saved your people,
the descendants of Jacob and of Joseph.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
16:
When the waters saw you, O God, they were afraid,
and the depths of the sea trembled.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
17: Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
18: Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
19: Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
20:
You led your people like a shepherd,
with Moses and Aaron in charge.
Comfort in Time of Distress#Ps 77 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.

1:
Listen, my people, to my teaching,
and pay attention to what I say.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
2:
I am going to use wise sayings
and explain mysteries from the past,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
3:
things we have heard and known,
things that our ancestors told us.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
4:
We will not keep them from our children;
we will tell the next generation
about the 
LORD
's power and his great deeds
and the wonderful things he has done.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
5:
He gave laws to the people of Israel
and commandments to the descendants of Jacob.
He instructed our ancestors
to teach his laws to their children,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
6:
so that the next generation might learn them
and in turn should tell their children.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
7:
In this way they also would put their trust in God
and not forget what he has done,
but always obey his commandments.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
8:
They would not be like their ancestors,
a rebellious and disobedient people,
whose trust in God was never firm
and who did not remain faithful to him.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
9:
The Ephraimites, armed with bows and arrows,
ran away on the day of battle.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
10:
They did not keep their covenant with God;
they refused to obey his law.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
11:
They forgot what he had done,
the miracles they had seen him perform.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
12:
While their ancestors watched, God performed miracles
in the plain of Zoan in the land of Egypt.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
13:
He divided the sea and took them through it;
he made the waters stand like walls.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
14:
By day he led them with a cloud
and all night long with the light of a fire.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
15:
He split rocks open in the desert
and gave them water from the depths.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
16:
He caused a stream to come out of the rock
and made water flow like a river.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
17:
But they continued to sin against God,
and in the desert they rebelled against the Most High.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
18:
They deliberately put God to the test
by demanding the food they wanted.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
19:
They spoke against God and said,
“Can God supply food in the desert?
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
20:
It is true that he struck the rock,
and water flowed out in a torrent;
but can he also provide us with bread
and give his people meat?”
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
21:
And so the 
LORD
 was angry when he heard them;
he attacked his people with fire,
and his anger against them grew,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
22:
because they had no faith in him
and did not believe that he would save them.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
23:
But he spoke to the sky above
and commanded its doors to open;
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
24:
he gave them grain from heaven,
by sending down manna for them to eat.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
25:
So they ate the food of angels,
and God gave them all they wanted.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
26:
He also caused the east wind to blow,
and by his power he stirred up the south wind;
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
27:
and to his people he sent down birds,
as many as the grains of sand on the shore;
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
28:
they fell in the middle of the camp
all round the tents.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
29:
So the people ate and were satisfied;
God gave them what they wanted.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
30:
But they had not yet satisfied their craving
and were still eating,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
31:
when God became angry with them
and killed their strongest men,
the best young men of Israel.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
32:
In spite of all this the people kept sinning;
in spite of his miracles they did not trust him.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
33:
So he ended their days like a breath
and their lives with sudden disaster.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
34: God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
35:
They remembered that God was their protector,
that the Almighty came to their aid.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
36:
But their words were all lies;
nothing they said was sincere.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
37:
They were not loyal to him;
they were not faithful to their covenant with him.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
38:
But God was merciful to his people.
He forgave their sin
and did not destroy them.
Many times he held back his anger
and restrained his fury.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
39:
He remembered that they were only mortal beings,
like a wind that blows by and is gone.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
40:
How often they rebelled against him in the desert;
how many times they made him sad!
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
41:
Again and again they put God to the test
and brought pain to the Holy God of Israel.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
42:
They forgot his great power
and the day when he saved them from their enemies
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
43:
and performed his mighty acts and miracles
in the plain of Zoan in the land of Egypt.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
44:
He turned the rivers into blood,
and the Egyptians had no water to drink.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
45:
He sent flies among them, that tormented them,
and frogs that ruined their land.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
46:
He sent locusts to eat their crops
and to destroy their fields.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
47:
He killed their grapevines with hail
and their fig trees with frost.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
48:
He killed their cattle with hail
and their flocks with lightning.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
; 78.48 hail… lightning; or terrible disease… deadly plague.
49:
He caused them great distress
by pouring out his anger and fierce rage,
which came as messengers of death.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
50:
He did not restrain his anger
or spare their lives,
but killed them with a plague.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
51:
He killed the firstborn sons
of all the families of Egypt.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
52:
Then he led his people out like a shepherd
and guided them through the desert.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
53:
He led them safely, and they were not afraid;
but the sea came rolling over their enemies.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
54:
He brought them to his holy land,
to the mountains which he himself conquered.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
55: God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
56:
But they rebelled against Almighty God
and put him to the test.
They did not obey his commandments,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
57:
but were rebellious and disloyal like their ancestors,
unreliable as a crooked arrow.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
58:
They angered him with their heathen places of worship,
and with their idols they made him furious.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
59:
God was angry when he saw it,
so he rejected his people completely.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
60:
He abandoned his tent in Shiloh,
the home where he had lived among us.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
; Josh 18.1; Jer 7.12–14; 26.6; 78.60 Shiloh: The central place of worship for the people of Israel before the time of King David.
61:
He allowed our enemies to capture the Covenant Box,
the symbol of his power and glory.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
62:
He was angry with his own people
and let them be killed by their enemies.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
63:
Young men were killed in war,
and young women had no one to marry.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
64:
Priests died by violence,
and their widows were not allowed to mourn.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
65:
At last the Lord woke up as though from sleep;
he was like a strong man excited by wine.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
66:
He drove his enemies back
in lasting and shameful defeat.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
67:
But he rejected the descendants of Joseph;
he did not select the tribe of Ephraim.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
68:
Instead he chose the tribe of Judah
and Mount Zion, which he dearly loves.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
69:
There he built his Temple
like his home in heaven;
he made it firm like the earth itself,
secure for all time.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
70:
He chose his servant David;
he took him from the pastures,
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
71:
where he looked after his flocks,
and he made him king of Israel,
the shepherd of the people of God.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.
72:
David took care of them with unselfish devotion
and led them with skill.
God and his People#Ps 78 Hebrew title: A poem by Asaph.

1:
O God, the heathen have invaded your land.
They have desecrated your holy Temple
and left Jerusalem in ruins.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
2:
They left the bodies of your people for the vultures,
the bodies of your servants for wild animals to eat.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
3: A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
4: A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
5:
, will you be angry with us for ever?
Will your anger continue to burn like fire?
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
6:
Turn your anger on the nations that do not worship you,
on the people who do not pray to you.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
7:
For they have killed your people;
they have ruined your country.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
8: A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
9: A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
10:
Why should the nations ask us,
“Where is your God?”
Let us see you punish the nations
for shedding the blood of your servants.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
11:
Listen to the groans of the prisoners,
and by your great power free those who are condemned to die.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
12:
Lord, pay the other nations back seven times
for all the insults they have hurled at you.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
13:
Then we, your people, the sheep of your flock,
will thank you for ever
and praise you for all time to come.
A Prayer for the Nation's Deliverance#Ps 79 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.

1: A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
2: A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
3:
Bring us back, O God!
Show us your mercy, and we will be saved!
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
4:
How much longer, 
LORD
 God Almighty,
will you be angry with your people's prayers?
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
5:
You have given us sorrow to eat,
a large cup of tears to drink.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
6:
You let the surrounding nations fight over our land;
our enemies insult us.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
7:
Bring us back, Almighty God!
Show us your mercy, and we will be saved!
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
8:
You brought a grapevine out of Egypt;
you drove out other nations and planted it in their land.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
9:
You cleared a place for it to grow;
its roots went deep, and it spread out over the whole land.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
10:
It covered the hills with its shade;
its branches overshadowed the giant cedars.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
11:
It extended its branches to the Mediterranean Sea
and as far as the River Euphrates.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
12:
Why did you break down the fences round it?
Now anyone passing by can steal its grapes;
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
13:
wild pigs trample it down,
and wild animals feed on it.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
14: A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
15:
Come and save this grapevine that you planted,
this young vine you made grow so strong!
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
16:
Our enemies have set it on fire and cut it down;
look at them in anger and destroy them!
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
17:
Preserve and protect the people you have chosen,
the nation you made so strong.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
18:
We will never turn away from you again;
keep us alive, and we will praise you.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.
19:
Bring us back, 
LORD
 God Almighty.
Show us your mercy, and we will be saved.
A Prayer for the Nation's Restoration#Ps 80 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph; a testimony.

1:
Shout for joy to God our defender;
sing praise to the God of Jacob!
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
2:
Start the music and beat the tambourines;
play pleasant music on the harps and the lyres.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
3:
Blow the trumpet for the festival,
when the moon is new and when the moon is full.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
4:
This is the law in Israel,
an order from the God of Jacob.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
5:
He gave it to the people of Israel
when he attacked the land of Egypt.
I hear an unknown voice saying,
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
6:
“I took the burdens off your backs;
I let you put down your loads of bricks.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
7:
When you were in trouble, you called to me, and I saved you.
From my hiding place in the storm, I answered you.
I put you to the test at the springs of Meribah.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
8:
Listen, my people, to my warning;
Israel, how I wish you would listen to me!
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
9: You must never worship another god.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
10:
I am the 
LORD
 your God,
who brought you out of Egypt.
Open your mouth, and I will feed you.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
11:
“But my people would not listen to me;
Israel would not obey me.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
12:
So I let them go their stubborn ways
and do whatever they wanted.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
13:
How I wish my people would listen to me;
how I wish they would obey me!
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
14:
I would quickly defeat their enemies
and conquer all their foes.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
15:
Those who hate me would bow in fear before me;
their punishment would last for ever.
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.
16:
But I would feed you with the finest wheat
and satisfy you with wild honey.”
A Song for a Festival#Ps 81 Hebrew title: By Asaph.

1:
God presides in the heavenly council;
in the assembly of the gods he gives his decision:
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
2:
“You must stop judging unjustly;
you must no longer be partial to the wicked!
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
3:
Defend the rights of the poor and the orphans;
be fair to the needy and the helpless.
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
4: Rescue them from the power of the wicked.
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
5:
“How ignorant you are! How stupid!
You are completely corrupt,
and justice has disappeared from the world.
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
6:
‘You are gods,’ I said;
‘all of you are children of the Most High.’
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
7:
But you will die like mortals;
your life will end like that of any prince.”
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.
8:
Come, O God, and rule the world;
all the nations are yours.
God the Supreme Ruler#Ps 82 Hebrew title: A psalm by Asaph.

1:

Look! Your enemies are in revolt,
